data.title: A Look At Packaging Corporation Of America (PKG) Valuation After Containerboard Price Hike And Share Reaction
data.description:
Packaging Corporation of America (PKG) has caught investor attention after announcing a $70 per ton containerboard price increase effective March 1, a move that coincided with a positive reaction in the shares.

See our latest analysis for Packaging Corporation of America.

The price hike comes on top of a steady run in the stock, with a 30 day share price return of 7.89% and a 90 day gain of 9.33%. At the same time, the 1 year total shareholder return is slightly negative, while the 3 and 5 year total shareholder returns of 76.93% and 94.31% point to stronger longer term momentum.

If this kind of pricing power story has your attention, it could be a good moment to widen your search and check out fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

With PKG trading at $225.39, only about 1% below the average analyst price target yet flagged with a large intrinsic discount, the question is obvious: is there still an opportunity here, or is future growth already priced in?

Advertisement

Most Popular Narrative: Fairly Valued

Packaging Corporation of America’s most followed narrative puts fair value at about $225.40, essentially in line with the last close at $225.39, which frames the current pricing debate.

Several updates point to 2026 as an inflection year, with anticipated benefits from integration synergies and containerboard pricing, supporting a view that current earnings estimates understate medium term profit potential.

Read the complete narrative.

Analysts are incorporating steady revenue growth, firmer margins, and a future earnings multiple that sits below today’s sector level. Curious how those elements combine to support this fair value assessment and what that suggests for the current price?

Result: Fair Value of $225.40 (ABOUT RIGHT)

data.title: Should Mycronic’s New Asian MMX Metrology Order Require Action From Mycronic (OM:MYCR) Investors?
data.description:
• Mycronic AB (publ) recently reported an order from a new Asian customer for its MMX metrology system, valued at US$2,000,000–US$4,000,000, with delivery scheduled in the current quarter.
  • The deal broadens Mycronic’s reach in the Asian semiconductor supply chain and highlights demand for tools serving mature semiconductor nodes and replacing legacy equipment.
  • We will now examine how this new MMX order from Asia shapes Mycronic’s investment narrative, particularly its positioning in mature semiconductor nodes.

Find companies with promising cash flow potential yet trading below their fair value.

Advertisement

What Is Mycronic's Investment Narrative?

For Mycronic, the core investment case still rests on a profitable, high‑margin equipment business with strong positions in mask writing and metrology, priced at a modest discount to peer and DCF fair values. The new MMX order in Asia fits neatly into that story rather than redefining it: at US$2,000,000–US$4,000,000, it looks immaterial to near term earnings, but it does slightly strengthen the short term order momentum ahead of upcoming results and underlines Mycronic’s relevance in mature semiconductor nodes. The more important near term catalysts remain quarterly earnings, dividend decisions, and the broader order pipeline across display and semiconductor customers. Key risks still center on lumpy order timing, dependence on a concentrated product set, and any cyclical pullback in capex.

However, one risk around order lumpiness and visibility is easy to underestimate. Despite retreating, Mycronic's shares might still be trading above their fair value and there could be some more downside. Discover how much.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Three Simply Wall St Community fair values cluster between SEK211.90 and SEK232.58, while the MMX-driven order momentum and ongoing reliance on cyclical equipment spending give very different angles for you to weigh.

data.title: Assessing Franklin Resources (BEN) Valuation After ETF Expansion And Tokenization Initiatives
data.description:

See our latest analysis for Franklin Resources.

These ETF launches and tokenization efforts come as Franklin Resources trades at US$25.31, with a 30-day share price return of 4.63% and 90-day share price return of 8.53%. Its 1-year total shareholder return of 32.04% contrasts with a 3-year total shareholder return decline of 5.04% and a 5-year total shareholder return of 21.20%, suggesting recent momentum has picked up after a weaker medium-term patch.

If these product and technology moves have your attention, it could be a good time to widen your watchlist and check out fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

data.title: Cathay Pacific Airways (SEHK:293) Valuation Check After Strong Passenger And Cargo Recovery
data.description:
Advertisement

Operating results put Cathay Pacific Airways (SEHK:293) in focus

Cathay Pacific Airways (SEHK:293) has attracted fresh attention after reporting December 2025 and full year traffic figures, with double digit year on year growth in both passenger volumes and cargo tonnage.

See our latest analysis for Cathay Pacific Airways.

The HK$12.58 share price has been supported by an 11.72% 90 day share price return and a 27.65% 1 year total shareholder return, suggesting momentum has been building around the recovery in passenger and cargo activity.

If Cathay Pacific’s rebound has caught your eye, you may wish to compare it with other aviation names through aerospace and defense stocks.

With HK$12.58 per share, a 51.10% intrinsic discount figure and a strong recent share price run, the key question is whether Cathay Pacific still trades below its underlying value or if markets are already pricing in future growth.

Most Popular Narrative: 11.7% Overvalued

The most followed narrative pegs Cathay Pacific Airways’ fair value at HK$11.26 per share, compared with the HK$12.58 last close, and builds its case around traffic recovery, margins and capital spending.

The analysts have a consensus price target of HK$10.378 for Cathay Pacific Airways based on their expectations of its future earnings growth, profit margins and other risk factors. However, there is a degree of disagreement amongst analysts, with the most bullish reporting a price target of HK$13.2, and the most bearish reporting a price target of just HK$8.2.

Read the complete narrative.

Want to see what sits behind that spread in outcomes? The narrative leans on measured revenue growth, slightly tighter margins and a higher future earnings multiple. The exact mix of those inputs is what drives the HK$11.26 figure.

Result: Fair Value of HK$11.26 (OVERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, if fleet upgrades and global network expansion lift earnings, or cargo growth proves more resilient, that could challenge the idea that Cathay Pacific is already overpriced.

Find out about the key risks to this Cathay Pacific Airways narrative.

Another View: Earnings Multiple Sends A Different Signal

While the HK$11.26 fair value points to Cathay Pacific as 11.7% overvalued, the current P/E of 8.6x tells a different story. It sits well below the Asian Airlines industry at 10.3x, the peer average at 25.9x, and an estimated fair ratio of 10.8x, which together suggest the market is pricing in a fair amount of uncertainty. The question is whether you think that discount reflects real long term risk or leaves room for a re rating if the recovery holds.

See what the numbers say about this price — find out in our valuation breakdown.

data.title: A Look At CareTrust REIT’s (CTRE) Valuation After Recent Analyst Upgrades And Rising Earnings Estimates
data.description:
Advertisement

Why recent analyst upgrades put CareTrust REIT (CTRE) on more investors’ radar

Recent analyst upgrades and new coverage with positive ratings have drawn fresh attention to CareTrust REIT (CTRE), as rising earnings estimates prompt investors to reassess the healthcare focused landlord’s stock.

See our latest analysis for CareTrust REIT.

The recent analyst upgrades come after a steady period for the share price, with a 90 day share price return of 4.36% and a year to date share price return of 1.49%. Long term total shareholder returns of 38.33% over one year and more than doubling over three and five years suggest momentum has been building rather than fading. Alongside the positive ratings, CareTrust REIT also recently outlined the tax status of its 2025 dividends, which may keep income focused investors attentive to the balance between ongoing distributions and the current US$36.85 share price.

If this healthcare REIT has caught your attention, it could be a good moment to widen your watchlist with other income and growth ideas across healthcare stocks.

With CTRE trading around US$36.85, an implied intrinsic discount of about 37% and a price target gap of roughly 12%, the key question is whether this reflects genuine mispricing or whether the market is already factoring in expectations for future growth.

Most Popular Narrative: 10.9% Undervalued

CareTrust REIT's most followed narrative pegs fair value at about $41.36 per share, a touch above the recent $36.85 close. This puts the current analyst upgrades in a clearer context.

The expanded investment pipeline of approximately $600 million, mainly in skilled nursing, seniors housing, and U.K. care homes, gives strong visibility into continued external growth, supporting FFO and dividend capacity. Rapid portfolio expansion and robust operator relationships across both the U.S. and U.K. are framed as a way to keep occupancy and rent collections resilient, which underpins the higher fair value estimate.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious what kind of revenue trajectory and margin profile are baked into that higher value? The narrative leans on ambitious growth, richer profitability, and a different earnings multiple than today. Want to see exactly which assumptions need to hold up to support a fair value above the current price?

Result: Fair Value of $41.36 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, this hinges on smooth integration of new acquisitions and steady tenant health, with reimbursement or regulatory changes in skilled nursing quickly challenging those assumptions.

Find out about the key risks to this CareTrust REIT narrative.

Another View: What The P/E Ratio Is Saying

Our fair value work points to CTRE trading at a discount, but the P/E ratio tells a more cautious story. At 31.6x earnings, the stock is richer than the global Health Care REITs average of 27x, yet cheaper than peer levels at 57.3x and below a fair ratio of 38.9x.

That mix of cheaper than peers but pricier than the wider industry hints at real valuation risk if growth or margins fall short, and potential upside if the market edges closer to the higher fair ratio. Which side of that trade off do you think current earnings can justify?

data.title: Estimating The Fair Value Of CAR Group Limited (ASX:CAR)
data.description:
Advertisement

Key Insights

  • The projected fair value for CAR Group is AU$30.68 based on 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity
  • With AU$29.70 share price, CAR Group appears to be trading close to its estimated fair value
  • The AU$38.87 analyst price target for CAR is 27% more than our estimate of fair value

Does the January share price for CAR Group Limited (ASX:CAR) reflect what it's really worth? Today, we will estimate the stock's intrinsic value by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting them to today's value. One way to achieve this is by employing the Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model. Believe it or not, it's not too difficult to follow, as you'll see from our example!

We generally believe that a company's value is the present value of all of the cash it will generate in the future. However, a DCF is just one valuation metric among many, and it is not without flaws. If you want to learn more about discounted cash flow, the rationale behind this calculation can be read in detail in the Simply Wall St analysis model.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

The Calculation

We're using the 2-stage growth model, which simply means we take in account two stages of company's growth. In the initial period the company may have a higher growth rate and the second stage is usually assumed to have a stable growth rate. In the first stage we need to estimate the cash flows to the business over the next ten years. Where possible we use analyst estimates, but when these aren't available we extrapolate the previous free cash flow (FCF) from the last estimate or reported value. We assume companies with shrinking free cash flow will slow their rate of shrinkage, and that companies with growing free cash flow will see their growth rate slow, over this period. We do this to reflect that growth tends to slow more in the early years than it does in later years.

Generally we assume that a dollar today is more valuable than a dollar in the future, so we discount the value of these future cash flows to their estimated value in today's dollars:

10-year free cash flow (FCF) forecast

2026202720282029203020312032203320342035
Levered FCF (A$, Millions) AU$428.6mAU$471.1mAU$529.8mAU$579.7mAU$619.1mAU$654.7mAU$687.5mAU$718.4mAU$748.1mAU$777.2m
Growth Rate Estimate SourceAnalyst x7Analyst x7Analyst x7Analyst x1Est @ 6.79%Est @ 5.74%Est @ 5.01%Est @ 4.50%Est @ 4.14%Est @ 3.89%
Present Value (A$, Millions) Discounted @ 8.1% AU$396AU$403AU$419AU$424AU$419AU$410AU$398AU$385AU$371AU$356

("Est" = FCF growth rate estimated by Simply Wall St)
Present Value of 10-year Cash Flow (PVCF) = AU$4.0b

The second stage is also known as Terminal Value, this is the business's cash flow after the first stage. The Gordon Growth formula is used to calculate Terminal Value at a future annual growth rate equal to the 5-year average of the 10-year government bond yield of 3.3%. We discount the terminal cash flows to today's value at a cost of equity of 8.1%.

Terminal Value (TV)= FCF2035 × (1 + g) ÷ (r – g) = AU$777m× (1 + 3.3%) ÷ (8.1%– 3.3%) = AU$17b

Present Value of Terminal Value (PVTV)= TV / (1 + r)10= AU$17b÷ ( 1 + 8.1%)10= AU$7.6b

The total value, or equity value, is then the sum of the present value of the future cash flows, which in this case is AU$12b. In the final step we divide the equity value by the number of shares outstanding. Relative to the current share price of AU$29.7, the company appears about fair value at a 3.2% discount to where the stock price trades currently. The assumptions in any calculation have a big impact on the valuation, so it is better to view this as a rough estimate, not precise down to the last cent.

The Assumptions

We would point out that the most important inputs to a discounted cash flow are the discount rate and of course the actual cash flows. If you don't agree with these result, have a go at the calculation yourself and play with the assumptions. The DCF also does not consider the possible cyclicality of an industry, or a company's future capital requirements, so it does not give a full picture of a company's potential performance. Given that we are looking at CAR Group as potential shareholders, the cost of equity is used as the discount rate, rather than the cost of capital (or weighted average cost of capital, WACC) which accounts for debt. In this calculation we've used 8.1%, which is based on a levered beta of 1.145. Beta is a measure of a stock's volatility, compared to the market as a whole. We get our beta from the industry average beta of globally comparable companies, with an imposed limit between 0.8 and 2.0, which is a reasonable range for a stable business.

View our latest analysis for CAR Group

SWOT Analysis for CAR Group

Strength
  • Debt is not viewed as a risk.
    Balance sheet summary for CAR.
Weakness
  • Earnings growth over the past year underperformed the Interactive Media and Services industry.
  • Dividend is low compared to the top 25% of dividend payers in the Interactive Media and Services market.
Opportunity
  • Annual earnings are forecast to grow faster than the Australian market.
  • Current share price is below our estimate of fair value.
Threat
  • Dividends are not covered by earnings.
  • Revenue is forecast to grow slower than 20% per year.
    See CAR's dividend history.

Moving On:

Whilst important, the DCF calculation shouldn't be the only metric you look at when researching a company. DCF models are not the be-all and end-all of investment valuation. Preferably you'd apply different cases and assumptions and see how they would impact the company's valuation. For instance, if the terminal value growth rate is adjusted slightly, it can dramatically alter the overall result. For CAR Group, there are three important items you should further research:

  1. Risks: Take risks, for example - CAR Group has 1 warning sign we think you should be aware of.
  2. Future Earnings: How does CAR's growth rate compare to its peers and the wider market? Dig deeper into the analyst consensus number for the upcoming years by interacting with our free analyst growth expectation chart.
  3. Other High Quality Alternatives: Do you like a good all-rounder? Explore our interactive list of high quality stocks to get an idea of what else is out there you may be missing!

PS. Simply Wall St updates its DCF calculation for every Australian stock every day, so if you want to find the intrinsic value of any other stock just search here.

data.title: Does Ventas’ EPS Surge And Uptrend Signal A Stronger Long-Term Thesis For VTR?
data.description:
• Recent updates indicate that Ventas has been reporting rapid earnings per share expansion, steady revenue growth and rising free cash flow, while consistently exceeding analyst expectations in past quarters.
  • Alongside these fundamentals, Ventas’ shares have been trading in a confirmed upward trend with a tight sideways consolidation pattern that may signal shifting investor positioning.
  • We’ll now look at how Ventas’ rapid earnings per share expansion shapes the company’s investment narrative and future risk-reward profile.

We've found 14 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

What Is Ventas' Investment Narrative?

To own Ventas, you need to believe that its recent return to profitability and improving cash generation can support both its dividend and ongoing reinvestment in healthcare real estate, even with a relatively low forecast return on equity and imperfect earnings quality. The latest earnings beat and guidance lift reinforce the near term catalyst of continued EPS expansion, while the share price’s steady uptrend and tight consolidation suggest the market is starting to price in that story more confidently rather than reacting to a one off headline. At the same time, the news does little to change the core risks: an income stream that has not always translated into fully covered interest and dividends, a valuation that already bakes in meaningful growth, and sensitivity to any disappointment versus those raised expectations.

However, one key cash flow risk behind the dividend story may surprise some investors. Despite retreating, Ventas' shares might still be trading 26% above their fair value. Discover the potential downside here.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Five fair value estimates from the Simply Wall St Community range from about US$33 to a very large outlier, showing how far apart private investors can be on Ventas. Set against that, the recent run of earnings beats and raised guidance keeps expectations high, which could amplify how the market reacts if growth or cash flows slip from here. This mix of dispersed valuations and elevated short term catalysts invites you to weigh several contrasting viewpoints before deciding where you stand.

data.title: How Investors May Respond To J.B. Hunt (JBHT) Earnings Beat, Buybacks And Dividend Increase
data.description:
• J.B. Hunt Transport Services recently reported fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 results showing higher net income and earnings per share despite slightly lower sales and revenue, while also completing over US$1.00 billion of share repurchases and authorizing a 2.3% increase in its regular quarterly dividend to US$0.45 per share.
  • Together with its adoption of Triumph’s automated payment solution, these moves highlight how J.B. Hunt is using technology, cost discipline, and capital returns to reinforce its role as a major North American transportation provider.
  • We’ll now examine how the earnings beat, supported by cost controls and buybacks, shapes J.B. Hunt’s investment narrative for investors.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 109 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10b in market cap - there's still time to get in early.

Advertisement

What Is J.B. Hunt Transport Services' Investment Narrative?

To own J.B. Hunt, you need to believe it can keep turning a flat freight backdrop into steady earnings progress by leveraging scale, technology and disciplined capital allocation. The latest quarter fits that story: revenue was essentially unchanged, but net income and EPS moved higher, helped by cost controls and a very large buyback program now totaling about US$1.00 billion. The new US$0.45 dividend, up 2.3%, reinforces a shareholder‑friendly approach and signals confidence in cash generation, even as the company carries a high debt load. In the near term, the key catalysts remain any firming in freight demand and evidence that efficiency investments like Triumph’s payment platform can support margins. The recent announcements support those themes, but they do not materially change the main risks around leverage, pricing pressure and insider selling.

However, investors should not overlook how that higher debt interacts with a still-weak freight market. J.B. Hunt Transport Services' shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued by 6%. Find out what it's worth.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Four Simply Wall St Community fair value estimates for J.B. Hunt span roughly US$153.55 to US$225.25, underscoring how far opinions can spread. Set against recent cost driven EPS growth, dividend increases and heavy buybacks, that dispersion invites you to weigh how much execution risk and freight market uncertainty you think the current price truly reflects.

Against that, the fresh US$60,000,000 Class 6 bond gives Loma Negra more breathing room, securing medium term funding at 6.50% and reducing immediate refinancing pressure rather than altering the core equity story. The recent launch of the NZ$160 million Mt Eden DOVA project and the resubmitted fast-track bid for the NZ$1 billion Downtown twin towers feed directly into that thesis, but they do not suddenly resolve the near-term pressure points that have weighed on the share price and financials. data.title: Could Diodes’ New Isolation and USB PD Chips Recast Its Edge in Industrial Power? (DIOD)
data.description:
• Earlier in January 2026, Diodes Incorporated launched its API772x RobustISO dual‑channel digital isolators and new AP5378x USB Type‑C Power Delivery controllers, targeting demanding applications from industrial automation and energy systems to high‑power portable devices.
  • The RobustISO isolators stand out by combining reinforced isolation ratings above 12kV with strong electromagnetic immunity and long projected operating lifetimes, positioning Diodes to compete in safety‑critical and noise‑prone end markets.
  • Next, we will examine how this push into higher‑isolation digital interfaces and USB PD3.1 power management could reshape Diodes' investment narrative.

What Is Diodes' Investment Narrative?

For Diodes, you really have to believe in a recovery story built on disciplined execution in niche power and signal products rather than headline-grabbing growth. The API772x RobustISO isolators and new USB PD3.1 controllers fit that thesis neatly, reinforcing Diodes’ push into industrial, energy and higher power portable designs where safety and efficiency matter. In the near term, these launches are more about strengthening the product roadmap and design‑win pipeline than moving the revenue needle, so they probably do not change the key catalysts: signs of margin improvement after a period of compressed profitability and how management allocates capital under the enlarged US$100.00m buyback. The main risk, now sharpened by these introductions, is whether Diodes can win and retain sockets in intensely competitive segments while its return on equity remains low and recent share price performance lags peers.

However, there is a risk that these new products do not translate into stronger profitability. Diodes' share price has been on the slide but might be dropping deeper into value territory. Find out whether it's a bargain at this price.

data.title: XD Inc. (SEHK:2400) Valuation Check After Launch Of Share Buyback Programme
data.description:
XD (SEHK:2400) has started buying back its own shares under a shareholder approved mandate, with capacity to repurchase up to 49,167,523 shares, representing 10% of its issued share capital.

See our latest analysis for XD.

XD’s share repurchase program arrives after a strong run, with a 30 day share price return of 27.89% and a 1 year total shareholder return of 243.35%, suggesting momentum has been building despite some shorter term swings.

If this kind of buyback fueled story has your attention, it could be a good moment to broaden your search and check out fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

With XD trading around HK$82.55 and an indicated intrinsic value gap of about 20%, plus fresh buybacks in motion, you have to ask: is there still mispricing here, or is the market already baking in future growth?

Advertisement

Price-to-Earnings of 26.2x: Is it justified?

XD trades on a P/E of 26.2x, which looks expensive next to both its Hong Kong Entertainment peers and the broader peer group, even after strong recent returns.

The P/E ratio compares the current share price with earnings per share, so a higher number usually reflects stronger earnings expectations or a willingness to pay up for profitability. For a game publisher and platform operator like XD, a richer P/E can sometimes go hand in hand with fast earnings growth and scalable digital economics.

Here, the tension is clear. XD has very strong recent earnings growth and an outstanding 45.2% return on equity. However, the P/E of 26.2x stands well above the Hong Kong Entertainment industry average of 13.6x and the peer average of 10.9x. It is also high compared to the estimated fair P/E of 15.3x, a level the market could move toward if expectations cool or growth normalises.

Explore the SWS fair ratio for XD

Result: Price-to-Earnings of 26.2x (OVERVALUED)

However, you still have to weigh risks such as XD’s P/E compressing toward the 15.3x fair level, or buybacks slowing if earnings momentum fades.

Find out about the key risks to this XD narrative.

Another View: DCF Points to Undervaluation

While the 26.2x P/E suggests XD looks expensive next to its industry and peer averages, our DCF model paints a different picture. With the shares at HK$82.55 versus an estimated fair value of HK$103.43, the model implies XD is trading at a meaningful discount. This raises the question of whether the earnings multiple is too harsh or the cash flow outlook is too generous.

Based on the latest data, Dmall trades on a P/S of 3.5x, which screens as mixed value when you line it up against both peers and an estimated fair level.

The P/S ratio compares the company’s market value to its revenue, and it is often used for loss making software names where earnings are not yet a guide. For Dmall, this matters because the business is still loss making, with revenue of CN¥1,998.27m and a reported loss of CN¥1,892.88m.

On one hand, Dmall is flagged as good value versus a peer average P/S of 4.2x. This suggests the market is not assigning the same revenue multiple as similar Hong Kong software companies. On the other hand, the SWS fair ratio framework marks Dmall as expensive versus an estimated fair P/S of 2.8x. data.description:

The ROSELLA news has arrived after a volatile stretch, with a 31.4% 7 day share price return lifting the stock to US$45.64. This suggests long term holders have still seen strong gains despite recent swings.

data.description:
• If you are wondering whether ASML Holding's share price still makes sense after its strong run, this article focuses squarely on what you are paying versus what you might be getting.
  • The stock last closed at US$1,413.35, with returns of 4.0% over 7 days, 31.8% over 30 days, 21.4% year to date, 106.5% over 1 year, 122.5% over 3 years and 164.9% over 5 years. This performance has put valuation firmly in the spotlight for many investors.
  • Recent commentary around ASML has centered on its role in the semiconductor supply chain and how investors are thinking about long term demand for chipmaking equipment. These themes help frame why the share price has moved the way it has and why valuation is now such a key talking point.
  • ASML currently has a valuation score of 1/6, based on how many of six checks suggest the shares look undervalued. Next we will look at what different valuation approaches are saying and then finish with a way to judge whether those numbers truly fit your own view of the business.

ASML Holding scores just 1/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: ASML Holding Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model takes estimates of a company’s future cash flows and discounts them back to today’s value, so you can compare that estimate with the current share price.

For ASML Holding, the model used here is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. It is based on cash flows in € rather than the US$ share price. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about €8.56b. Analysts have provided forecasts for several years, and Simply Wall St extrapolates further ahead, with projected free cash flow of €20.08b in 2030. The ten year path in the model runs from around €8.80b in 2026 up to €29.83b in 2035, with each year discounted back to today.

Putting all those discounted cash flows together gives an estimated intrinsic value of about US$916.26 per share, compared with the recent share price of US$1,413.35. On this basis, the DCF implies the shares trade at roughly a 54.3% premium to the modelled value, so the stock screens as overvalued on this measure.

Result: OVERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests ASML Holding may be overvalued by 54.3%. Discover 884 undervalued stocks or create your own screener to find better value opportunities.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for ASML Holding.

Approach 2: ASML Holding Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company like ASML Holding, the P/E ratio is a useful way to think about what you are paying for each unit of current earnings. It helps you compare the share price to the underlying profit, which is often the starting point for many investors.

A “normal” or “fair” P/E tends to be higher when investors expect stronger earnings growth and lower business risk, and lower when growth expectations are modest or risk is higher. With ASML, the current P/E is 48.07x. That sits above the Semiconductor industry average of 40.81x and below the peer group average of 53.54x, so the stock is priced at a premium to the wider industry but not to the highest end of its peer set.

Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for ASML is 33.58x. This is a proprietary estimate of what the P/E might look like after factoring in earnings growth, profitability, risk profile, industry and market cap, rather than just lining it up against simple peer or industry averages. Because it is built around ASML’s specific characteristics, it aims to be a more tailored yardstick than broad comparisons. On this basis, the current P/E of 48.07x sits well above the Fair Ratio of 33.58x, which points to the shares looking expensive on this measure.

Result: OVERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1425 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your ASML Holding Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so let us introduce you to Narratives. These let you attach a clear story to your numbers by linking your view of ASML Holding’s business to assumptions about future revenue, earnings and margins, and then to an estimated fair value.

On Simply Wall St, within the Community page used by millions of investors, a Narrative is your personal, easy to set up framework that connects what you believe about the company to a financial forecast, and then to a fair value that you can directly compare with today’s share price.

That comparison, Fair Value versus current Price, can help you decide whether you see ASML Holding as a potential opportunity, something to hold, or an investment you might want to avoid. Your Narrative will keep updating automatically as new information such as news, guidance or earnings is added to the platform.

For ASML Holding, one investor might build a Narrative with a relatively high fair value that reflects strong expectations for future demand for chipmaking equipment, while another might use much more cautious revenue and margin assumptions that produce a much lower fair value. Both investors can clearly see how their story, numbers and decisions connect.

Do you think there's more to the story for ASML Holding? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!

This article by Simply Wall St is general in nature. We provide commentary based on historical data and analyst forecasts only using an unbiased methodology and our articles are not intended to be financial advice. data.title: New York Tipping Ruling Tests Uber Eats Economics And Investor Expectations
data.description:
• A federal judge has rejected Uber and DoorDash's attempt to block New York City's new tipping law for food delivery apps.
  • The rule requires platforms to present a tipping option at checkout for customers ordering through these apps.
  • The law is set to take effect in New York City, affecting the way Uber Eats and rival services handle tipping flows.
  • Delivery platforms now face new compliance requirements and potential shifts in customer behavior and courier earnings.

For Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER), this ruling affects a core part of its business: the Uber Eats delivery segment in one of its largest urban markets. Food delivery has become a key pillar alongside the ride hailing operation, and New York City is an important test bed for regulatory ideas that can influence other regions. For investors, this adds another data point to how gig-economy rules are evolving for large app based platforms.

Looking ahead, you may want to watch how Uber adjusts app design, tipping prompts, and courier pay structures in response to the law. The way customers respond to the mandated tip screen, and how earnings for delivery workers are affected, could inform future policy debates and set reference points for other cities considering similar rules.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Uber Technologies by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Uber Technologies.

Is Uber Technologies financially strong enough to weather the next crisis?

Advertisement

Quick Assessment

  • ✅ Price vs Analyst Target: At US$81.98, the share price sits below the US$110.43 analyst price target range midpoint.
  • ✅ Simply Wall St Valuation: Shares are described as trading at 56.0% below an estimated fair value.
  • ✅ Recent Momentum: The 30 day return of 0.89% is modest but in positive territory.

Check out Simply Wall St's in depth valuation analysis for Uber Technologies.

Key Considerations

  • 📊 The New York City tipping rule directly affects Uber Eats unit economics and how much of each order reaches couriers.
  • 📊 Watch any updates to guidance or disclosures on delivery segment margins, order volumes and average tip per order in this market.
  • ⚠️ There are 2 identified major risks, including forecasts for earnings to decline by an average of 4.8% per year. Further regulation of gig work could add pressure.

Dig Deeper

data.title: Equinox Gold Refocuses On North America With Record Output And Upside Potential
data.description:
• Equinox Gold (TSX:EQX) has completed the sale of its Brazilian mines Aurizona, RDM, and the Bahia Complex to CMOC Group.
  • The company is using the proceeds to reduce debt and sharpen its focus on North American gold operations.
  • Equinox Gold has restructured a gold offtake agreement with Vox Royalty, shifting deliveries from Brazilian assets to the Greenstone mine in Ontario.
  • The company has reported record annual gold production and highlighted contributions from the Greenstone and Valentine projects.

For investors watching gold miners, Equinox Gold (TSX:EQX) is now more concentrated in Canada and the United States, with Brazil no longer part of its mine portfolio. The company remains a pure gold producer, which can appeal to investors who prefer direct exposure to the metal rather than diversified commodity exposure.

The key assets to monitor are Greenstone in Ontario and Valentine, which the company expects to contribute to production. The combination of a refocused asset base, a revised royalty offtake and record annual production places greater attention on how efficiently Equinox Gold can operate and expand its North American operations.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Equinox Gold by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Equinox Gold.

How Equinox Gold stacks up against its biggest competitors

Quick Assessment

  • ✅ Price vs Analyst Target: At CA$22.39, the share price sits below the CA$25.05 analyst target range midpoint.
  • ✅ Simply Wall St Valuation: Shares are flagged as undervalued, trading about 25% below the estimated fair value.
  • ✅ Recent Momentum: The 30 day return of roughly 12.2% shows recent positive price momentum.

Check out Simply Wall St's in depth valuation analysis for Equinox Gold.

Key Considerations

  • 📊 The Brazil exit and focus on North American gold assets tighten the story around Greenstone and Valentine as core drivers.
  • 📊 Keep an eye on production volumes, costs per ounce, debt reduction progress, and how the Greenstone offtake restructuring flows through to cash generation.
  • ⚠️ Shareholders have faced substantial dilution over the past year, which is important when weighing any future capital needs against potential returns.

Dig Deeper

data.title: WEILONG Delicious Global Holdings Ltd (HKG:9985) Not Flying Under The Radar
data.description:
WEILONG Delicious Global Holdings Ltd's (HKG:9985) price-to-earnings (or "P/E") ratio of 23x might make it look like a strong sell right now compared to the market in Hong Kong, where around half of the companies have P/E ratios below 12x and even P/E's below 7x are quite common. However, the P/E might be quite high for a reason and it requires further investigation to determine if it's justified.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Recent times have been advantageous for WEILONG Delicious Global Holdings as its earnings have been rising faster than most other companies. It seems that many are expecting the strong earnings performance to persist, which has raised the P/E. You'd really hope so, otherwise you're paying a pretty hefty price for no particular reason.

Check out our latest analysis for WEILONG Delicious Global Holdings

Keen to find out how analysts think WEILONG Delicious Global Holdings' future stacks up against the industry? In that case, our free report is a great place to start.
Advertisement

How Is WEILONG Delicious Global Holdings' Growth Trending?

There's an inherent assumption that a company should far outperform the market for P/E ratios like WEILONG Delicious Global Holdings' to be considered reasonable.

If we review the last year of earnings growth, the company posted a worthy increase of 11%. Pleasingly, EPS has also lifted 378% in aggregate from three years ago, partly thanks to the last 12 months of growth. Accordingly, shareholders would have probably welcomed those medium-term rates of earnings growth.

Shifting to the future, estimates from the twelve analysts covering the company suggest earnings should grow by 16% per annum over the next three years. That's shaping up to be materially higher than the 14% each year growth forecast for the broader market.

With this information, we can see why WEILONG Delicious Global Holdings is trading at such a high P/E compared to the market. Apparently shareholders aren't keen to offload something that is potentially eyeing a more prosperous future.

The Bottom Line On WEILONG Delicious Global Holdings' P/E

Generally, our preference is to limit the use of the price-to-earnings ratio to establishing what the market thinks about the overall health of a company.

As we suspected, our examination of WEILONG Delicious Global Holdings' analyst forecasts revealed that its superior earnings outlook is contributing to its high P/E. At this stage investors feel the potential for a deterioration in earnings isn't great enough to justify a lower P/E ratio. It's hard to see the share price falling strongly in the near future under these circumstances.

You always need to take note of risks, for example - WEILONG Delicious Global Holdings has 1 warning sign we think you should be aware of.

If P/E ratios interest you, you may wish to see this free collection of other companies with strong earnings growth and low P/E ratios.
